description: Hardening defaults for WebSocket / Socket.IO / SignalR / Phoenix Channels endpoints. CORS does not apply to WebSockets — Origin validation must be done explicitly at upgrade time.

auth_modes_recommended: cookie + Origin allowlist (with CSRF mitigation via Origin)
short-lived JWT in Sec-WebSocket-Protocol subprotocol header
signed query parameter token (HMAC, 5-10 minute lifetime)

anti_patterns.rule: Missing Origin allowlist on the upgrade handshake enables Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking
Authenticating via post-upgrade message instead of on the handshake means the connection is opened with cookies before auth — CSWSH risk
Plain ws:// in production exposes tokens and content on path
No per-message size limit enables single-message memory exhaustion
No per-connection message rate limit enables real-time spam / DoS
No per-IP / per-user connection limit enables socket exhaustion
No ping/pong heartbeat leaves half-open connections on LBs and intermediaries
Client reconnect without exponential backoff + jitter melts the server during outages
Echoing pod / shard / build IDs in real-time messages aids attacker recon
Authorization checked only on handshake fails when user is logged out / role changed mid-session; re-check on privileged messages
